Two
                                                                                                                     The book contains 4 parts and 20 chapters. I have concentrated on
                                                                                                                     four parts, namely, (i) the logical basis of development-oriented
                                                                                                                     finance, (ii) the theoretical contents of development-oriented finance,
                                                                                                                     (iii) the operation mechanism of development-oriented finance, and
                                                                                                                     (iv) the reform of development-oriented financial institute. I hope
                                                                                                                     that this study will contribute to the research of China’s modern
                                                                                                                     financial institution, serving China for better development. There are five chapters in Part 1, which discusses the logic foun-
                                                                                                                     dation of development-oriented finance. It includes the following:
                                                                                                                     (1) China should seize the main contradiction, especially the main
                                                                                                                     contradiction in the mid- and long-term economic development. In
                                                                                                                     the initial stage of socialism in China, the economy developed under
                                                                                                                     the background of resource restrictions where social demand
                                                                                                                     exceeded the total demand without proper limits. This kind of devel-
                                                                                                                     opment is on the stage of tight economic operation. The deepest and
                                                                                                                     most central reason of tight macroeconomic operation is defective
                                                                                                                     and backward of the credit system.

The lagged credit system cannot
                                                                                                                     meet China’s huge demand of industrialization and urbanization,
                                                                                                                     rapid progress in infrastructure construction, the upgrading of the
